summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of the historic 1987 New Year's Eve concert, highlighted by scorching versions of Bertha, Bird Song, Wharf Rat and Knockin' on Heaven's Door.
review: The SBD recording is clean and well‑balanced, offering the best audio source for this marathon NYE show. Fans rave about several standout performances—Bertha, Cold Rain & Snow, Bird Song, Music Never Stopped, Wharf Rat, Not Fade Away and Knockin' on Heaven's Door—while noting a few minor hiccups such as a lyric slip in Push Comes to Shove and some edits in the broadcast. Overall the musical flow across three sets feels electric and the extra interview/skit material adds historic flavor.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews consistently praise the performance and list many standout songs; recording is described as the best‑quality SBD available, with only minor technical quirks.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard (SBD) praised as the highest‑quality source; some broadcast edits noted (missing Promised Land intro, 10‑sec edit in Space) and occasional missing instrument bleed on SBD, but overall clear and balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set marathon typical of a NYE celebration, with a warm opener, high‑energy second set and a festive third set featuring guest appearances; pacing keeps momentum despite long runtime.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar shines on Wharf Rat and Space (teases Too early), plus a memorable yell at Bobby during the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil is noted for his involvement in the multi‑track mix; no specific performance comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (Bobby) leads the missing Promised Land intro and delivers strong vocals throughout, especially on Bird Song and other numbers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keys) “really smokes” during Iko Iko and adds notable texture to several songs.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Graham’s entrance is highlighted; Mickey is described as good‑natured after years of jokes about his appearance.
